question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/mirror_node_client.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a network-adjacent position perform a man-in-the-middle attack against the plaintext HTTP request to cfg.mirrorRest (no TLS enforcement) to return a crafted nodesEnvelope with rogue node endpoints, causing the SDK client to route fee-bearing CryptoTransfer transactions to attacker-controlled nodes that impose fees outside design parameters?
